use std::sync::{Arc, Condvar, Mutex};
use std::thread;
use std::time::Duration;

fn wait_for_ready_cv(signal: Arc<(Mutex<bool>, Condvar)>) {
    let (lock, cvar) = &*signal;
    let mut ready = lock.lock().unwrap();
    while !*ready {
        ready = cvar.wait(ready).unwrap();
    }
}

fn poll_with_exponential_backoff() {
    let mut delay = Duration::from_millis(10);
    let max_delay = Duration::from_secs(5);
    loop {
        match check_service_health() {
            Ok(()) => break,
            Err(_) => {
                thread::sleep(delay);
                delay = (delay * 2).min(max_delay);
            }
        }
    }
}

async fn async_wait_with_notify() {
    let (tx, rx) = tokio::sync::watch::channel(false);
    tokio::spawn(async move {
        tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_secs(1)).await;
        let _ = tx.send(true);
    });
    let mut rx = rx;
    while !*rx.borrow() {
        rx.changed().await.unwrap();
    }
}

fn check_service_health() -> Result<(), ()> { Err(()) }

fn main() {
    poll_with_exponential_backoff();
}
